title: rubus luxurians
intro: Rubus luxurians, also known as the New Zealand blackberry or mountain blackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Rubus luxurians include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, salves, and ointments. Additionally, the plant's leaves and roots have been used to make poultices and compresses to treat skin conditions and wounds.
